name: User Access Review
description: User access to systems and data is periodically reviewed to ensure access remains appropriate and aligned with each individual's role. User access reviews reduce the risk of unauthorised or excessive access by confirming that only authorised individuals retain access and that access is revoked when no longer required.

attributes.text: Access reviews are performed on a periodic basis (e.g. quarterly)
testable_criteria: Evidence shows an access review was performed for the in-scope system during the period under review.
The review was completed at a frequency of at least quarterly (i.e. no more than 3 months between consecutive reviews).
The review covers all users with access to the in-scope system as of the review date.
The review has a documented completion date.

attributes.text: Access is reviewed and approved by an appropriate system or data owner
testable_criteria: The reviewer is identified by name and role on the review evidence.
The reviewer is the designated system or data owner (or an appropriate delegate) for the in-scope system.
The reviewer is independent of the users being reviewed (i.e. did not self-approve their own access).
There is documented evidence of approval (e.g. sign-off, ticket, signed spreadsheet) from the reviewer.

attributes.text: Inappropriate or excessive access identified during the review is remediated in a timely manner
testable_criteria: All access flagged as inappropriate or excessive during the review is identified in the review evidence.
For each flagged item, there is evidence that access was modified or revoked.
Remediation was performed in a timely manner following the review's completion date.
Evidence of remediation (e.g. ticket, system screenshot, deprovisioning log) is retained and traceable to the flagged item.
